name: Colorful 2-bedroom house
subtitle: Colorful house in the hills of Tenerife
tagline: Your cosy mountain retreat in Valleseco
welcomeMessage: Welcome to our colourful house in the hills of Tenerife! Nestled in the charming village of Valleseco, it is full of character and surrounded by nature. We hope you love it as much as we do. Reach out anytime if you need anything at all.

address.display: Calle Manuel Ravina 69, Valleseco, Santa Cruz de Tenerife
address.nearestLandmark: Near Punto y Coma Minimarket, Valleseco village

checkin.keyLocation: Look for the blue door on the upper part of the house — the keybox is right next to it.
steps: Park at the recommended parking area (there is always space, and you avoid almost any stairs)
From the parking, walk toward the end of the street, then take the stairs down on the left side
At the next street below, turn right — this is the street of the house
Walk straight, cross the small garden with cacti and plants, and continue between the houses
After about 1 minute, on the left you will see a white house with a blue door
It is the upper part of the house — take the stairs next to the house to reach the entrance
Find the blue door with the keybox next to it. Code: 1214
checkin.notes: If you have any trouble finding us, call or WhatsApp anytime, we are happy to guide you step by step. Note: Google Maps is not 100% accurate for the last stretch, so follow the photo guide!

gettingHere.byCar: Park at the recommended spot (maps link below) — there are always spaces and you avoid almost any stairs. Then follow the walking directions in the photo guide. ⚠️ Google Maps is not 100% accurate for the last stretch, so please use the photo guide!
gettingHere.parking: Recommended parking: https://maps.app.goo.gl/iZAoE6vizeim4x3K6 — spaces are always available, and it is a short flat walk to the house entrance.
gettingHere.publicTransport: Take bus 909, 910, or 916 from Santa Cruz de Tenerife. Get off at Valleseco — it is a small village with just one stop. Find the Punto y Coma Minimarket and take the stairs on the left, about 20 metres after passing it. It is a 5-10 minute uphill walk to the house. ⚠️ Follow the photo guide for the last stretch as Google Maps is not fully accurate.

directionPhotos.caption: Recommended parking area. There is always space and you avoid almost any stairs.
From the parking, walk in this direction until the street ends, then take the stairs down on the left side.
Go down to the next street and turn right. This is the street of the house.
Walk straight and cross the garden with cacti and other plants.
After about 1 minute, on the left you will see a white house with a blue door.
It is the upper part of the house. Take the stairs right next to the house to reach the entrance.
The blue door with the keybox next to it. Code: 1214

gettingHere.taxi: Taxis are available from Tenerife North (TFN) and Tenerife South (TFS) airports. Agree on the fare before departing or ensure the meter is running.

houseRules: Please keep smoking to the balconies and terraces only
Please keep it peaceful. Quiet hours are 11pm to 8am. No parties or events
We love animals, but we're unable to accommodate pets. Sorry!
Please use the bin next to the toilet instead of flushing paper, Cyprus plumbing really can't handle it
Place the metallic key in the electricity plug by the door when you're inside, and take it with you when you leave
Please return the keys to the keybox and reset the code when you leave. If keys are lost or locked in, a locksmith call-out will be at your cost, so do take care of them!
Please treat the apartment as your own, and let us know right away if anything gets damaged. We understand accidents happen

appliances.name: Electricity
appliances.instructions: Pop the metallic key into the plug by the door to power the apartment, and remember to take it with you when you head out. Each socket also has its own wall switch: if something isn't turning on, that's usually why. EU adapters available if needed.

appliances.name: Kitchen
appliances.instructions: Your living room has a small kitchen area with an electric stove, a small electric oven with grill function, and a high-performance blender. Please feel free to use them all, just be sure to clean them well afterwards (especially the blender). The house's electrical system is a little old, so it can't handle several high-power appliances running at once, for example a hair dryer plus the oven plus the stove. To be on the safe side, please use just one of these at a time. If the electricity ever cuts out, don't worry at all, just let us know as soon as you can and we'll help sort it out. ⚠️ It's always summer in Tenerife, so the bugs and insects never sleep! We rarely have any problems, as long as you clear away all leftover food and pop it straight in the bin. We'd also recommend washing the dishes right after eating to keep flies and other insects from showing up later.

appliances.name: Washing Machine
appliances.instructions: You'll find the washing machine in the kitchen, please feel free to use it. The fast programme called "Rapido" is usually all you need. If you have any questions, just let us know!

appliances.name: Shower & Hot Water
appliances.instructions: Water is heated by solar panels on the roof, so it's warm and eco-friendly! Give it 2-3 minutes to come through, that's just the water making its way down from the panels. On overcast or cloudy days the solar may not be enough: press the boiler button next to the bathroom door and wait 20-30 minutes per person before showering. ⚠️ Always switch the boiler off before stepping in.

appliances.name: Drinking Water
appliances.instructions: Tap water in Tenerife is said to be safe and clean, though most people prefer bottled water, which you'll find at any supermarket or kiosk. The cheaper and more practical option is to grab a 5L, 8L or larger gallon.

appliances.name: Bathroom
appliances.instructions: You'll find fresh towels, a hair dryer, and soap and shampoo all ready for you. If anything else is missing, just let us know and we'll sort it out. ⚠️ The toilet flush can be a little temperamental now and then: if the water doesn't stop running, simply press the button one more time and it will settle.

appliances.name: The Magical Terrace
appliances.instructions: This is the exact same terrace you saw in the photos! Because of Tenerife's wonderfully unpredictable micro climate (hello rain, wind and sunshine, sometimes all in one day! 🌧️💨☀️), we can't leave the pillows, covers and rugs outside, so you'll first find it looking a little bare with a covered BBQ. The good news: everything you need to make it cosy and beautiful is waiting for you, either in the beige closet on the terrace or inside the apartment. Feel free to set it up however you like and simply enjoy! 🏝️🪴

appliances.name: BBQ
appliances.instructions: Up on the terrace you'll find the BBQ, which runs on charcoal. We don't usually keep charcoal in the house unless previous guests have left some, but you can pick up everything you need at any supermarket or local mini market for a lovely grill afternoon or evening. 🙂 ⚠️ Please remember to give the BBQ a good clean after using it, and make sure it's fully out. Thank you so much! 🙏

appliances.name: Plants
appliances.instructions: If you're staying a few days, the plants would love a little water every 3-4 days. They'll appreciate it!

appliances.name: Mari, the Lovely Neighbour
appliances.instructions: Our right hand and greatest help is the lovely Mari, who lives right next door. If anything comes up at all (say you need an extra towel), she's always happy to help. She might even pop by with some homemade sweets if she has the time! Mari speaks only Spanish, but don't worry if you don't, somehow it always works out beautifully. 🙂

items.name: Playa de Valleseco / Los Charcos de Valleseco
items.tip: Closest swim, quick dip, local feel. Closest option — a small urban gravel beach with calm waters and easy access. Practical rather than pretty, but great for a quick dip.

items.name: Playa de Las Teresitas
items.tip: Best easy beach day, families, swimming. Golden sand, full facilities, restaurants, and calm water protected by a breakwater. The safest top recommendation from this location.

items.name: Playa de Las Gaviotas
items.tip: Black sand, quieter beach, hidden gem. A wilder black sand beach close to Las Teresitas. Good when you want something less polished and less crowded, but the sea can be rougher.

items.name: Playa de Igueste de San Andrés
items.tip: Quiet village, scenic coast, hidden gem. A beautiful local escape beyond San Andrés. Better for a scenic coastal visit than a classic sandy beach day.

items.name: Playa de Antequera
items.distance: Accessible by boat or long hike
items.tip: Remote beach, adventure, wild nature. One of the true hidden gems of Anaga. Not practical for a casual visit, but absolutely amazing for adventurous guests.

items.name: Playa del Roque de las Bodegas
items.tip: Scenic drive, surf, restaurants nearby. A great Anaga day trip stop — beautiful black sand and dramatic mountain scenery, with restaurants close by. Better for scenery and food than calm swimming.

items.name: Playa de Almáciga
items.tip: Wild black sand, surfers, nature. Beautiful and dramatic, but not the easiest swimming beach. Best for guests who love wild coastlines and don't expect sunbeds.

items.name: Playa de Benijo
items.tip: Sunset, photography, wild Anaga scenery. One of the most beautiful beaches in Tenerife for photos and sunsets — wild, black sand, with strong ocean conditions. Check sea conditions before going.

items.name: Bajamar Natural Pools
items.tip: Safe sea pools, families, wave watching. Not a beach but very worth it. Great when the north coast is dramatic and you want a safer ocean experience.

items.name: Punta del Hidalgo Natural Pools
items.tip: Natural pools, coastal walk, sunset. An excellent coastal day trip — more about swimming pools, lava coast, and stunning views than sand.

items.name: Playa de Mesa del Mar
items.tip: Black sand, local beach, north coast vibe. A nice alternative on the north coast, good for guests exploring Tacoronte or the wine area.

items.name: Playa de El Bollullo
items.tip: Beautiful black sand, north coast classic. One of the prettiest black sand beaches in northern Tenerife. Worth it if you are already visiting La Orotava or Puerto de la Cruz.

items.name: Playa Jardín, Puerto de la Cruz
items.tip: Puerto de la Cruz day trip. Normally famous and scenic, but water quality closures have affected Playa Jardín since 2024. Check current bathing status before going.

items.name: Playa de Valleseco coastal walk
items.tip: Closest sea walk, local feel, quick stroll. The easiest coastal walk from the house. Practical rather than polished, but very local and good for a quick sea view.

items.name: Rambla de Santa Cruz
items.distance: 2-3 km, car, taxi, bus, or long walk
items.tip: City walk, trees, cafes, local life. A pleasant urban walk through one of the main green boulevards of Santa Cruz. Lovely for a relaxed morning or late afternoon.

items.name: Parque García Sanabria
items.distance: 3-4 km, car, taxi, bus, or long walk
items.tip: Shaded walk, plants, fountains, sculptures. One of the best easy scenic walks in the city, perfect when you want greenery without leaving Santa Cruz.

items.name: Plaza de España & Castillo de San Cristóbal
items.distance: 3.5-4.5 km, car, taxi, bus, or walk
items.tip: City history, central square, easy sightseeing. A great starting point for exploring central Santa Cruz. The underground interpretation centre preserves remains of the old castle, capital of Tenerife since 1833.

items.name: Iglesia de Nuestra Señora de la Concepción & La Noria
items.distance: 4-5 km, car, taxi, bus, or walk
items.tip: Old streets, church, bars, local evening walk. Lovely for a historical city stroll. The church and nearby La Noria streets are among the most atmospheric parts of old Santa Cruz.

items.name: Mercado Nuestra Señora de África
items.tip: Local market, food, architecture, morning walk. Not a classic monument, but a great cultural stop. Best in the morning for local produce, snacks, and atmosphere.

items.name: Auditorio de Tenerife
items.tip: Modern architecture, photos, sea walk. One of Santa Cruz's most iconic modern landmarks, designed by Santiago Calatrava. Stunning for photos along the seafront.

items.name: Palmetum of Santa Cruz
items.tip: Botanical walk, sea views, unusual garden. A very good scenic walk inside the city. A 12 hectare botanical garden specialising in palm trees.

items.name: San Andrés village
items.tip: Village feel, seafood, Las Teresitas combo. Small, simple, and local. Great to combine with Las Teresitas beach or a drive into Anaga.

items.name: Mirador de Las Teresitas
items.tip: Best beach viewpoint, photos, scenic stop. One of the easiest 'wow view' stops from the house. Best combined with Las Teresitas or San Andrés.

items.name: San Cristóbal de La Laguna Historic Centre
items.distance: 13-16 km, car, taxi, tram, or bus
items.tip: UNESCO town, colonial streets, architecture. One of the best cultural excursions from Santa Cruz. La Laguna's historic centre has been a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1999.

items.name: Anaga Rural Park scenic drive
items.distance: 20-35 km depending on stop, car or tour
items.tip: Mountains, laurel forest, viewpoints, villages. The best nature and scenery excursion from this location. Anaga is a protected rural park and, since 2015, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve.

items.name: Cruz del Carmen Visitor Centre, Anaga
items.tip: Forest walks, Anaga information, viewpoints. The best starting point for Anaga forest walks, with information and advice for routes in the Rural Park.

items.name: Taganana village
items.tip: Historic village, Anaga scenery, mountain road. A beautiful old village in Anaga with dramatic landscapes. Great for a scenic drive plus a more traditional feel.

items.name: Roque de las Bodegas scenic coast
items.tip: Ocean views, black sand, seafood lunch. More scenic than historical, but excellent for an Anaga day trip. Combine with Taganana, Almáciga, and Benijo.

items.name: Benijo viewpoint and coastal walk
items.tip: Sunset, wild coast, photography. One of the most dramatic scenic spots in Tenerife. Better for views and photos than easy swimming.

items.name: Kings Avenue Mall
items.tip: Shopping, food court, cinema. Practical for an indoor break on hot or very windy days.

items.name: Paphos Harbour
items.tip: One of the easiest evening plans from Avlida. Dinner, drinks, and a relaxed walk by the sea.

items.name: Paphos Aphrodite Waterpark
items.tip: Best family attraction nearby. Easy half-day or full-day option on hot days.

items.name: Coral Bay & Corallia Beach
items.tip: Easiest classic beach excursion. Sand, sunbeds, and facilities. Hard to go wrong.

items.name: Horse Riding
items.tip: Nice alternative to beach and history. Book in advance, especially in high season.

items.name: Adonis Baths Waterfalls
items.tip: Popular excursion, road can be rough. Better for adventurous guests than polished sightseeing.

items.name: Sea Caves & Edro III Shipwreck
items.tip: Easy scenic excursion. Great for photos and sunset. Combine with Oniro by the Sea.

items.name: Paphos Zoo
items.tip: Good family option near Coral Bay. Easy to combine with Sea Caves or a beach day.

items.name: Avakas Gorge
items.tip: One of the best nature walks in the region. Wear proper shoes. Avoid after heavy rain or extreme heat.

items.name: Wine Villages around Kathikas
items.tip: Great for adults wanting a local experience. Better with a tour if everyone is tasting.

items.name: Akamas Jeep Safari
items.tip: Best for guests who want wild nature without driving difficult roads themselves. One of the best preserved areas in Cyprus.

items.name: Polis & Latchi
items.tip: Calmer, more local side of Cyprus. Good for lunch, a coastal walk, and Blue Lagoon boat departures.

items.name: Blue Lagoon Boat Trip from Latchi
items.tip: One of the best full-day excursions from Paphos. Many tours combine with Akamas and Baths of Aphrodite.

items.name: Baths of Aphrodite
items.tip: Small site, but beautiful surroundings. Best combined with Latchi, Blue Lagoon, and Polis.

items.name: Omodos Village
items.tip: One of the prettiest village excursions from Paphos. Works well as part of a Troodos or wine route day.

items.name: Kourion Archaeological Site
items.tip: Excellent day trip toward Limassol. Ancient theatre with sea views is the highlight.

items.name: Limassol Marina & Old Town
items.tip: More urban and polished than Paphos. Best combined with Kourion or Omodos.

items.name: Troodos Mountains & Villages
items.tip: Best full-day escape from the coast. Villages, monasteries, wine routes, and cooler air.

items.name: Paphos Coastal Walkway
items.tip: Best no-car option. Walk from the Tombs of the Kings area toward Lighthouse Beach, the harbour, and Paphos Castle.

items.name: Lighthouse Beach Coastal Walk
items.tip: Practical from Avlida. Good for guests who want nature without a proper hike.

items.name: Tombs of the Kings coastal loop
items.tip: Combines ancient tombs, open landscape, and coastline. Not a formal trail, but a very nice loop.

items.name: Sea Caves coastal walk, Peyia
items.tip: Easy scenic outing. Combine with Edro III Shipwreck and Oniro by the Sea.

items.name: Avakas Gorge Nature Trail
items.tip: Best nature trail near Paphos. Follows Avgas River through limestone formations. Wear proper shoes. Avoid after heavy rain.

items.name: Aphrodite's Rock coastal walk
items.tip: Scenic stop, not a long hike. Good when driving toward Limassol or the airport.

items.name: Lara Beach & Toxeftra coastal area
items.tip: Beautiful but rough access. Wild coast and turtle nesting areas. Respect the environment.

items.name: Smigies Nature Trail, Neo Chorio
items.tip: Less demanding than Aphrodite or Adonis. Short route 2.5 km (1hr), long route 6 km (2hrs).

items.name: Baths of Aphrodite short walk
items.tip: Gentle nature stop, not a real hike. Best combined with Latchi or a Blue Lagoon boat trip.

items.name: Aphrodite Nature Trail, Akamas
items.tip: One of the most beautiful hikes in the region. Wide views over Polis, Latchi, and Akamas.

items.name: Adonis Nature Trail, Akamas
items.tip: Views over Polis Chrysochous, Paphos Forest, and Akamas. Connects with Smigies and Aphrodite trails.

items.name: Troodos Nature Trails
items.tip: Best full-day hiking option in summer when the coast is too hot. Cooler forest trails and mountain scenery.

items.name: Karlina Restaurant
items.tip: Easiest no-car dinner option near Avlida. Ranked among the top restaurants near Tombs of the Kings.

items.name: Dias Zeus Greek Restaurant
items.tip: Reliable nearby option on Tombs of the Kings Avenue. Greek and Mediterranean with strong guest ratings.

items.name: Tea for Two
items.tip: Good for an easy breakfast or lunch close to home. Friendly service and great value.

items.name: Psarou Fish Tavern
items.tip: Cozy tavern known for fresh fish, traditional meze, and a friendly local vibe.

items.name: Kingfisher Taverna
items.tip: Small, relaxed taverna serving high-quality fish dishes and local favourites in a more upscale setting.

items.name: Three Little Pigs
items.tip: Local favourite with high-quality food, great service, and a lovely atmosphere. Their house wine is a treat!

items.name: Kings Avenue Mall Food Court
items.tip: Not special, but very practical. Good for families, quick food, coffee, or escaping the heat.

items.name: Old Town Paphos cafés
items.tip: More local feeling than the harbour. Nice for brunch, coffee, or early evening drinks.

items.name: Pallia Ilektriki
items.tip: A good Old Town option. Ranked among popular places near Old Town Paphos.

items.name: Ta Pinakia
items.tip: Good if you want something less harbour-focused. Well-rated near Old Town Paphos.

items.name: O Firis
items.tip: Modern twist on Cypriot cuisine with generous portions. Live music almost every evening. Book ahead!

items.name: Paphos Harbour restaurants
items.tip: Touristy but reliable. Many options for dinner, drinks, and a sunset walk by the sea.

items.name: King's Restaurant
items.tip: Popular harbour area choice. Good fish and Cypriot food.

items.name: Theo's Restaurant
items.tip: Good for seafood near the harbour. Sea bass, octopus, prawns, and calamari.

items.name: The Moorings Café Restaurant
items.tip: More about the harbour atmosphere than fine dining. Easy and relaxed.

items.name: West End Hotel & Restaurant
items.tip: Family-run with generous portions and delicious seafood, steps from the sea.

items.name: Oniro by the Sea
items.tip: One of the best scenic spots in the Paphos area. Combine with Sea Caves and Edro III shipwreck.

items.name: Kathikas wine village restaurants
items.tip: More authentic than the seafront. Best for a longer lunch or early dinner with wine tasting.

items.name: Latchi Harbour tavernas
items.tip: Best combined with a Blue Lagoon boat trip, Baths of Aphrodite, or a Polis day trip.

items.name: Lidl
items.tip: Budget-friendly with a wide range of groceries. Great for stocking up on basics.

items.name: AlphaMega
items.tip: Large supermarket with a wide variety of fresh produce and local Cypriot items.

items.name: Kings Avenue Mall
items.tip: The main shopping mall in Paphos. Wide range of shops, restaurants and a cinema.

items.name: Paphos Market
items.tip: Covered marketplace with stalls for clothing, bags, jewelry, beach gear and local souvenirs.

items.name: Old Town
items.tip: Wander through the beautiful Old Town and discover many small boutique stores and artisan shops.

eatAndDrink.tip: Easiest no-car dinner option near Avlida. Ranked among the top restaurants near Tombs of the Kings.

eatAndDrink.tip: Reliable nearby option on Tombs of the Kings Avenue. Greek and Mediterranean with strong guest ratings.

shopping.name: Kings Avenue Mall
shopping.tip: The main shopping mall in Paphos. Wide range of shops, restaurants and a cinema.

shopping.name: Paphos Market
shopping.tip: Covered marketplace with stalls for clothing, bags, jewelry, beach gear and local souvenirs.

shopping.name: Old Town
shopping.tip: Wander through the beautiful Old Town and discover many small boutique stores and artisan shops.

gettingAround.name: Bus
gettingAround.info: Central bus station (Tombs of the Kings) is 18 min walk (~1.3 km) away, with direct connections to main attractions, the airport, and nearby towns. Bus stops have recently been added to Google Maps too, though always worth checking against the official website for the latest timetables.

gettingAround.name: RideNow (Car Sharing)
gettingAround.info: Car-sharing app that works well around Paphos. Sign up in minutes. Cars typically within a 5–10 min walk.

gettingAround.name: Bolt
gettingAround.info: Budget-friendly ride app. Good alternative to metered taxis around Paphos.

gettingAround.name: Taxi
gettingAround.info: Various taxi services are available online. One reliable option is Paphos Taxi Airport, where you can also arrange a pickup directly via WhatsApp: +357 99 460 829.

contacts.name: Emergency Services
contacts.note: Police, Ambulance, Fire. Free from any phone

contacts.name: Paphos District Hospital
contacts.note: Main hospital in Paphos, approx. 10 min drive

emergency.nearestHospital: Paphos District Hospital, approx. 10 min drive.
emergency.nearestPharmacy: Several pharmacies within walking distance on the main road. Ask us if you need a recommendation.

steps: If you could give any dishes a quick wash and leave them on the drying rack, that would be great!
If you could clear out the fridge and pop any leftover food in the bin, we'd appreciate it!
If you could take the rubbish bags to the bins in the parking area — on your right as you walk out past the pool — that would be a big help!
Before heading off, please lock all windows, balcony doors and shutters.
Please switch off all lights, AC, and the TV when you're done.
When you're ready to go, please lock up and return all keys to the keybox, resetting the code after.
Drop us a WhatsApp to let us know you've left — and we'd truly love a review if you enjoyed your stay!
checkout.lateCheckout: We try to be flexible wherever we can. If you'd like a late checkout, please let us know 1-2 days before your departure and we'll do our best to make it happen.
